relations.subject: 锈色歌百灵
relations.evidence: |year=2024 |title=Calendulauda rufa |volume=2024 |article-number=e.T22717101A263888587 |doi=10.2305/IUCN.UK.2024-2.RLTS.T22717101A263888587.en |access-date=2 August 2025}} | genus = Calendulauda | species = rufa | authority = (Lynes, 1920) | subdivision_ranks = Subspecies | subdivision = See text | synonyms = | range_map = Mirafra rufa distribution map.png }} The rusty bush lark (Calendulauda rufa), also known as the rusty lark, is a species of lark in the family Alaudidae found in the Sahel region of north-central Africa

relations.subject: 锈色歌百灵
relations.evidence: It is typically found inhabiting the dry savannah ecoregions of Chad, Mali, Niger, Sudan, and Togo. It occurs in dry savanna with scattered open Combretum woodland. Despite being listed by IUCN as 'Least Concern', none were seen between May 1931 and 2 February 2026, when one was seen in south-central Chad; this sighting resulted in the first live photographs of the species ever taken
